rationale: A visible force concentration near the Tethys border will deter further aggression and exploit our current military advantage despite high crisis levels.
Disabling Tethyan command-and-control systems will offset our energy dependency and degrade their operational coordination.
Severing maritime supply routes will amplify economic pressure from sanctions and weaken Tethys's war-sustaining capacity.
Covert disruption of key energy infrastructure used by Tethys will asymmetrically exploit their reliance on Novaris-linked pipelines.
Proactively neutralizing Tethyan agents prevents leaks that could undermine military and political operations amid internal ethnic tensions.
Orchestrating a plausible provocation can shift international narrative and justify escalation if diplomatic conditions deteriorate.
Securing critical reserves now mitigates the 75% sanctions impact and buffers against total pipeline disruption through Tethys.
Devaluing Tethys's currency through targeted financial actions would exacerbate their domestic economic fragility under sanctions.
Mobilizing domestic capital through patriotic financing offsets revenue loss and sustains long-term crisis readiness without immediate fiscal collapse.
A precision strike against a high-value, non-civilian Novaris military target will demonstrate resolve and disrupt operational readiness without triggering full escalation.
Conducting visible air and naval exercises near contested zones deters further Novaris advances by signaling defensive cohesion.
Disabling key command-and-control nodes in Novaris’s cyber infrastructure exploits their vulnerability and offsets our conventional weakness.
Covertly disrupting energy export infrastructure used by Novaris cripples their revenue stream and amplifies domestic unrest.
Amplifying narratives of government incompetence among Novaris’s ethnic Tethyan population deepens societal fractures.
Intelligence-enabled precision operations against mid-level Novaris military commanders degrade coordination and morale.
Rallying democratic allies with shared strategic interests can break our diplomatic isolation and increase pressure on Novaris.
Proposing neutral-party mediation demonstrates international law compliance and exposes Novaris as the obstructionist power.
Establishing monitored aid routes in contested areas wins global sympathy and highlights Novaris’s humanitarian violations.
Pre-positioning critical war materiel and energy reserves offsets sanctions pressure and extends operational endurance.
Securing alternative export routes through neutral partners reduces dependency on Novaris-controlled transit.
Issuing state-backed war bonds mobilizes domestic capital and signals economic resilience to both population and adversaries.
